{{神社 |名称=名居神社 |
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|37|17|N|136|7|36|E}}}} |画像= |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図=Japan Mie|緯度度=34|緯度分=37|緯度秒=17|経度度=136|経度分=7|経度秒=36 |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。 [[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。 == 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年([[599年]])[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地である。 == 祭神 == [[大巳貴命]]・[[ないの神|なゐの神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://momijiaoi.net/nai/|access-date=2025-9-3}} == 境内社 == 興玉神社 山神社{{Cite web|title=名居神社（名張市）|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-3}} == 脚注 == {{DEFAULTSORT:ないしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている。<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 脚注 == <references />

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神と称されていた<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:ないしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の[[惣社]]であった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

[[JA:名居神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q135039259}}

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の[[惣社]]であった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == <references />

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

[[JA:名居神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q135039259}}

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の[[惣社]]であった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == <references />

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の[[惣社]]であった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:ないしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==

[[JA:名居神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q135039259}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|名居神社|version=107479350|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の[[惣社]]であった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == <references />
[[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]]
==End Japanese==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}} {{神社 |名称=名居神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} |所在地=[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知2092 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大巳貴命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |例祭=11月3日 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[名張市]]下比奈知に[[鎮座]]する神社。
[[式内社]]で、旧社格は[[村社]]。
== 歴史 == 創祀は、不詳。[[日本書紀]]の推古天皇紀に[[推古天皇]]7年（[[599年]]）[[大和地方]]を中心とした大地震（[[推古地震]]）があり、その後諸国に「[[ないの神|なゐの神]]（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の[[伊賀国]]の神社。[[元伊勢]]の伝承地でもある。
[[江戸時代]]に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（[[宇流富志禰神社]]の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の[[惣社]]であった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[大己貴命]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[天児屋根命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]・[[事代主命]]・[[蛭子命]]<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>
== 境内社 == *興玉神社 - 祭神：[[猿田彦神]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[火之迦具土神]]・[[大物主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref> *山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神<ref>{{Cite web|title=名居神社|url=https://www.genbu.net/data/iga/nai_title.htm|access-date=2025-9-27}}</ref>
== 遙拝所 == 皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == <references />
==End Japanese== [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]]

'''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|名張市|Q166183}}下比奈知に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社。

{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創祀は、不詳。{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}の推古天皇紀に{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}7年（{{jalink|599年|Q34050}}）{{jalink|大和地方}}を中心とした大地震（{{jalink|推古地震|Q17218310}}）があり、その後諸国に「{{jalink|ないの神|Q10928456|なゐの神}}（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の{{jalink|伊賀国|Q1047128}}の神社。{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の伝承地でもある。

{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（{{jalink|宇流富志禰神社|Q135039260}}の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の{{jalink|惣社}}でもあった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===

{{wikidata link|Q135039259}}

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}}
{{神社
|名称=名居神社
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}}
|所在地={{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|名張市|Q166183}}下比奈知2092
|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{jalink|大巳貴命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
|創建=不詳
|例祭=11月3日
|地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|名張市|Q166183}}下比奈知に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社。

{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創祀は、不詳。{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}の推古天皇紀に{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}7年（{{jalink|599年|Q34050}}）{{jalink|大和地方}}を中心とした大地震（{{jalink|推古地震|Q17218310}}）があり、その後諸国に「{{jalink|ないの神|Q10928456|なゐの神}}（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の{{jalink|伊賀国|Q1047128}}の神社。{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の伝承地でもある。

{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（{{jalink|宇流富志禰神社|Q135039260}}の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の{{jalink|惣社}}でもあった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|少彦名命}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}・{{jalink|事代主命}}・{{jalink|蛭子命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== 境内社 ===
*興玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|火之迦具土神}}・{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|安徳天皇|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
*{{要出典範囲|山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神|date=2025年12月}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== 遙拝所 ===
皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}
*{{jalink|三重県の神社一覧|Q48761540}}

創祀は、不詳。{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}の推古天皇紀に{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}7年（{{jalink|599年|Q34050}}）{{jalink|大和地方}}を中心とした大地震（{{jalink|推古地震|Q17218310}}）があり、その後諸国に「{{jalink|ないの神|Q10928456|なゐの神}}（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の{{jalink|伊賀国|Q1047128}}の神社。{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の伝承地でもある。

{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（{{jalink|宇流富志禰神社|Q135039260}}の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の{{jalink|惣社}}でもあった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|少彦名命}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}・{{jalink|事代主命}}・{{jalink|蛭子命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== 境内社 ===
*興玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|火之迦具土神}}・{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|安徳天皇|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
*{{要出典範囲|山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神|date=2025年12月}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== 遙拝所 ===
皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}
*{{jalink|三重県の神社一覧|Q48761540}}

=== 脚注 ===
<references />

{{出典の明記|date=2025年10月}}
{{神社
|名称=名居神社
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}}
|所在地={{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|名張市|Q166183}}下比奈知2092
|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{jalink|大巳貴命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
|創建=不詳
|例祭=11月3日
|地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=名居神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''名居神社'''（ないじんじゃ/なゐじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|名張市|Q166183}}下比奈知に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社。

{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創祀は、不詳。{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}の推古天皇紀に{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}7年（{{jalink|599年|Q34050}}）{{jalink|大和地方}}を中心とした大地震（{{jalink|推古地震|Q17218310}}）があり、その後諸国に「{{jalink|ないの神|Q10928456|なゐの神}}（地震の神）」を祀らせた中の{{jalink|伊賀国|Q1047128}}の神社。{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の伝承地でもある。

{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に著された「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85-3069406 伊水温故]」では、名居神社は宇奈根社（{{jalink|宇流富志禰神社|Q135039260}}の旧称）とされている<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>。なお、江戸時代は、国津大明神とも称されており、比奈知川上流に散在していた国津神社の{{jalink|惣社}}でもあった。<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|少彦名命}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}・{{jalink|事代主命}}・{{jalink|蛭子命}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== 境内社 ===
*興玉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|火之迦具土神}}・{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|安徳天皇|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
*{{要出典範囲|山神社 - 祭神：山ノ神|date=2025年12月}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== 遙拝所 ===
皇大神宮[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 遙拝]所・橿原神宮遙拝所

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}
*{{jalink|三重県の神社一覧|Q48761540}}

=== 脚注 ===

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=October 2025}}
{{神社
|名称=Nawino Shrine
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}}
|所在地={{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}} Shimohinachi 2092

|創建=unknown
|例祭=November 3
|地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Nawino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Shimohinachi, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}.

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{jalink|599|Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{jalink|Yamato region}} ({{jalink|Suiko earthquake|Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{jalink|Nai no kami|Q10928456|Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{jalink|Iga Province|Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{jalink|Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ebisu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Emperor Antoku|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}}
* {{jalink|List of shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Shimohinachi, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}.

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{jalink|599|Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{jalink|Yamato region}} ({{jalink|Suiko earthquake|Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{jalink|Nai no kami|Q10928456|Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{jalink|Iga Province|Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{jalink|Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ebisu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Emperor Antoku|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}}
* {{jalink|List of shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540}}

=== Notes ===
<references />

{{出典の明記|date=October 2025}}
{{神社
|名称=Nawino Shrine
|画像={{画像募集中|cat=名張市}}
|所在地={{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}} Shimohinachi 2092
|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神={{jalink|Ōnamuchi|Q702140|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}
|社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}
|創建=unknown
|例祭=November 3
|地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Nawino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Shimohinachi, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}.

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{jalink|599|Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{jalink|Yamato region}} ({{jalink|Suiko earthquake|Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{jalink|Nai no kami|Q10928456|Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{jalink|Iga Province|Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{jalink|Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ebisu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Emperor Antoku|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===
* {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}}
* {{jalink|List of shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540}}

=== Notes ===
<references />

{{DEFAULTSORT:ないしんしや}}

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Shimohinachi, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}.

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{jalink|599|Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{jalink|Yamato region}} ({{jalink|Suiko earthquake|Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{jalink|Nai no kami|Q10928456|Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{jalink|Iga Province|Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{jalink|Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Ebisu-no-Mikoto}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{jalink|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} / {{jalink|Emperor Antoku|Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

* {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540|WD=Q48761540}}

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}}
* {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540|WD=Q48761540}}

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===

* {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Mie Prefecture|Q48761540|WD=Q48761540|1=List of shrines in Mie Prefecture}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===

}}
'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okitama Shrine - enshrined deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} / {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} / {{ill|lt=Emperor Antoku|Q316651|WD=Q316651|1=Emperor Antoku}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=名張市史|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/212|access-date=2025-9-3}}</ref>
* {{要出典範囲|Yama Shrine - enshrined deity: Yama-no-Kami|date=December 2025}}<!-- 出典にならない個人サイトを貼り続けるのはおやめください -->

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.

=== See also ===

{{nihongo|'''Nawino Shrine'''|名居神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iga Province]].

'''Nawino Shrine''' (ないじんじゃ / なゐじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimohinachi, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.

=== History ===
Its original foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' (Chronicle of Empress Suiko), in {{ill|lt=599|Q34050|WD=Q34050|1=599}} (Suiko 7), a major earthquake centered on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato region|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Suiko earthquake|Q17218310|WD=Q17218310|1=599 Suiko earthquake}}) occurred, after which shrines for "{{ill|lt=Nai no kami|Q10928456|WD=Q10928456|lt=Nai no kami|1=Nai no Kami}}" (deity of earthquakes) were enshrined in various provinces. This is identified as the shrine in {{ill|lt=Iga Province|Q1047128|WD=Q1047128|1=Iga Province}} among those. It is also a traditional site of {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}.

In ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E6%B0%B4%E6%B8%A9%E6%95%85 Isui Onko]'', written in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Nawino Shrine is described as Una-ne-sha (former name of {{ill|lt=Urufushine Shrine|Q135039260|WD=Q135039260|1=Unanenofushimino Shrine}})<ref>{{Cite web|title=伊水温故|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/9571119/1/189|access-date=2025-9-4}}</ref>. In the Edo period it was also called Kunitsu Daimyōjin and served as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} for Kunitsu shrines scattered in the upper reaches of the Hinachi River<ref>{{Cite web|title=県神社庁|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=3028|access-date=2025-11-20}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=名張市史 下巻 |publisher=名張地方史研究会 |page=388-389}}</ref>

=== Precinct shrines ===

=== Remote worship sites ===
Remote worship site for Kōtaijingū [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%81%99%E6%8B%9D-2091014 yōhai], and remote worship site for Kashihara Jingū.
